revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:石園座多久虫玉神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Isononimasu Takumushitama Shrine |image =File:Iwazono Niimasutakumushitama Shrine.JPG |location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|qid=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}} Katasu-cho 15-33 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 28.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 44 | 経度秒 = 33.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | map国コード = JP-29 |deity= {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|qid=Q11572681|1=Tamakushi-hime}}; {{ill|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|1=Toyotama-hime}}; {{ill|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|1=Watatsumi}} |Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（大）
旧{{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |alternative name='''Ryuo-gu''' |festival=[[October 9]] |festival=Rice Planting festival |main hall=[[Kasuga-zukuri]] |map=Japan Nara |location = {{ill|Yamatotakada|qid=Q838669|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.507778|N|135.7425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} }} [[File:石園坐多久虫玉神社 大和高田市片塩町 Isono-ni-masu-takumushitama-jinja 2013.8.16 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Torii]] {{Nihongo|'''Isononimasu-takumushitama Shrine'''|石園座多久虫玉神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|qid=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada|1=Yamatotakada, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-taisha|ja|式内大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai-taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9610964}}, and its former Rank was {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Locally, it is also known as '''Ryuo-gu''' and is revered by the community. There is a tradition that links {{ill|Yokooji (Nara)|ja|横大路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokooji (Nara)|1=Yokooji (Nara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11542429}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|qid=Q705542|lt=Omiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|qid=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} as forming the head, body, and tail of a dragon, respectively. == Deities == [[File:Takumushitama-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|thumb|Shrine in the precincts]] The main deities enshrined are Tatatsu-tamahiko no Mikoto and {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|qid=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, along with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1474187}} and {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2329261}}. Tatatsu-tamahiko no Mikoto is identified with Kamotamayorihiko no Mikoto, enshrined at the Haji-no-Omiya Shrine, a subsidiary of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|qid=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} (Kamigamo Shrine) and the Ki no Shita Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}. There is also a theory that the original deity of this shrine is Takutsuhiko no Mikoto, with the character "虫" being a misrecording of "豆" in Japanese. According to the book 'Yamato no Kami Matsuri' by Shōzō Tanaka, the original deities were Takutsuhiko no Mikoto and {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}}. == History == [[File:Legendary Imperial Palace of Emperor Annei.jpg|thumb|Stone monument of the legendary palace site of Emperor Annei]] == Overview == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but it is located on the traditional site of the palace of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}, known as Katashio Ukatanomiya. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, an imperial festival was held at this shrine. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} records the shrine as "Yamato Province Katsushimo District Isononimasu Takumushitama Shrine Two Shrines," listing it as a grand shrine and noting that it received offerings for monthly and harvest festivals. It is believed that the shrine was historically known as "Isono-kami." There is an interest in the relationship with {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|qid=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Jingu|1=Isonokami Jingū}}. Another theory suggests a connection with {{ill|Sonokami Shrine|ja|園神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonokami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. In 1966 (Shōwa 41), it was listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Shrine Rankings === * January 27, 859 (Tian'an 3), promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - recorded as "Isonon Takumushitama Shrine" == Access == * Train: Get off at Takadashi Station on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|qid=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, then a short walk. * Road: {{ill|Japan National Route 166|en|Japan National Route 166|qid=Q11421414|lt=Japan National Route 166|1=Japan National Route 166}} == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|qid=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Isononimasu-takumushitama jinja}} *[http://yamatoji.nara-kankou.or.jp/01shaji/01jinja/02west_area/ryuogu-isononimasutakumushitamajinja/ Ryuo-gu (Isononimasu Takumushitama Shrine)] - Nara Travel Network {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|石園座多久虫玉神社|version=102009130|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石園座多久虫玉神社]] {{Commons category|Isononimasu-takumushitama jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11585337}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
